agency: New York City Business Integrity Commission (BIC)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/bic (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and the transactional portal at bicportal.nyc.gov (identified as Salesforce Experience Cloud via server: sfdcedge + x-sfdc-request-id + the Lightning community /s/ path scheme, e.g. /s/viopay). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Business Integrity Commission (BIC)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 9 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: BIC is a split domain: an informational NYC.gov site (Akamai/nginx/Livesite/Dynatrace) plus a licensing/enforcement portal that is a Salesforce Experience Cloud app (bicportal.nyc.gov, server: sfdcedge) with no API.

The regulatory registry is unusually OPEN: 9 NYC Open Data datasets covering trade waste hauler licensees (867j-5pgi, 78k views, BIC's most-viewed asset), broker/self-hauler/construction-and-demolition registrants, public wholesale-market businesses (Hunts Point, Fulton Fish), the licensee/registrant vehicle fleet, issued violations, complaints, and denied companies.

But the business SERVICE layer has no machine-readable surface: applying for or renewing a license/registration and paying a violation fine (/s/viopay) live only inside the login-walled Salesforce portal or on paper.

The open data publishes the OUTPUTS of the licensing lifecycle (who is licensed, who was denied, what violations issued) but offers no way to TRANSACT it — a public registry with a private front counter.
Net-new write surface: apply for a trade waste license/registration (TradeWasteLicenseApplication) — no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 9 BIC datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open BIC API — regulatory registry, violations, complaints, and fleet data.
apply/renew a license or registration; pay a violation fine (/s/viopay). Salesforce Lightning community — server: sfdcedge, x-sfdc-request-id; JavaScript-only, no JSON/OpenAPI.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
BIC complaints about trade waste hauling / wholesale markets route through 311; only closed records reach Open Data.

fruit.name: Trade Waste Hauler Licensees
fruit.notes: BIC's core licensed account — companies licensed to collect/remove/dispose of trade waste. Keyed on BIC NUMBER; carries recycling/organics authorizations, effective/expiration dates, full geography spine.

fruit.name: Registrants (Broker, Self-Hauler, C&D)
fruit.notes: Three parallel registration streams (the lighter-touch credential vs. a full license). Same shape; distinguished by industry. Keyed on BIC NUMBER.

fruit.name: Public Wholesale Market Businesses
fruit.notes: Businesses registered to operate in the public wholesale markets — Hunts Point Produce, Hunts Point Meat, Fulton Fish. Keyed on BIC NUMBER; MARKET names the market.

fruit.name: Licensee / Registrant Fleet (Vehicles)
opendata_match: Licensees and Registrants Fleet Information n84m-kx4j (18c)
fruit.notes: BIC-plated hauler trucks — make/model/year, fuel, GVWR, and side-guard safety compliance. Joined to operator by BIC NUMBER; identified by BIC PLATE NUMBER.

fruit.name: BIC Issued Violations
fruit.notes: Enforcement actions — rule code, counts, fine amount/maximum, OATH/settlement disposition and dates. Joined to the cited account by BIC NUMBER; keyed on VIOLATION NUMBER.

fruit.name: Complaints & Inquiries
fruit.notes: Complaints/inquiries about regulated accounts, including vehicle detail. INTAKE runs through 311 and the BIC complaints form; only closed/aggregate records reach Open Data (partial coverage of a live workflow).

fruit.name: Denied Companies (application decisions)
opendata_match: Denied TW and Wholesale Market Companies exsg-kpya (22c)
fruit.notes: The only public trace of the application lifecycle — companies DENIED a license/registration, with application status and date. The upstream apply/review/approve steps have no Open Data twin and no API.

fruit.name: Pay a violation fine
fruit.notes: Fine payment lives only in the Salesforce portal (/s/viopay); no API. A locked service-layer transaction.

fruit.name: Renew a license / registration
fruit.notes: Renewals are handled in the Salesforce portal; no API. Renewal status only becomes public if the account appears (or is denied) in the registry snapshots.

fruit.name: Apply for a trade waste license/registration
fruit.notes: The core BIC business transaction — apply for a license, registration, or exemption. No API and no full Open Data twin (only denials surface, via exsg-kpya); lives only in the Salesforce Experience Cloud portal or on paper. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) + Salesforce Experience Cloud portal (bicportal.nyc.gov)
meta.netnew: TradeWasteLicenseApplication — apply for a trade waste license/registration
meta.tagline: BIC's regulatory registry is the most open of any domain yet, but every business transaction is locked inside a Salesforce portal.
meta.headline: A public registry with a private front counter: the licensee, violation, and complaint data is wide open, yet applying, renewing, and paying have no API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Two managed platforms — NYC.gov Livesite + Salesforce Experience Cloud — modern but the transaction layer is a rented SaaS.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content or portal API; the informational site and Salesforce portal expose no machine-readable surface.
scorecard_notes.openData: 9 rich datasets — licensees, three registrant streams, markets, fleet, violations, complaints, denials — high traffic.
scorecard_notes.coreData: The core object (licensee/registration) is fully published as an open registry keyed on BIC NUMBER.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API — apply, renew, and pay-a-fine all locked in the Salesforce portal or on paper.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; the OpenAPI + MCP artifact here are the proposal.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Open data is city-owned, but the licensing system-of-record and its transactions are locked in a vendor SaaS with no export/API.
